channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Virginia approved a plan to draw four more seats that lean heavily for Democrats
item.description: Virginia voters approved mid-decade redistricting that aims to help Democrats win 10 of the state's 11 seats in Congress. Republicans are challenging the map, but Democrats are calling it a victory.
item.content:encoded: Virginia voters approved mid-decade redistricting that aims to help Democrats win 10 of the state's 11 seats in Congress. Republicans are challenging the map, but Democrats are calling it a victory.
item.dc:creator: Jahd Khalil

item.title: DOJ accuses Southern Poverty Law Center of using donations to pay secret informants
item.description: The Justice Department has accused the Southern Poverty Law Center of money laundering and other crimes. The DOJ says the Alabama-based civil rights organization paid sources to stoke racial hatred.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department has accused the Southern Poverty Law Center of money laundering and other crimes. The DOJ says the Alabama-based civil rights organization paid sources to stoke racial hatred.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: A former South Korean spy confronts the hidden war that never ended
item.description: More than 70 years after the Korean War, South Korea is still confronting the legacy of a secret conflict waged in the shadows.
item.content:encoded: More than 70 years after the Korean War, South Korea is still confronting the legacy of a secret conflict waged in the shadows.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Is political gerrymandering the future of U.S. politics? Virginia's governor weighs in
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks to Democratic Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger about a measure she supported — that voters approved Tuesday — to redraw the state's congressional maps to favor Democrats.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks to Democratic Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger about a measure she supported — that voters approved Tuesday — to redraw the state's congressional maps to favor Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Bartlam

item.title: Knitting enthusiasts from around the country recently met for yarn fest
item.description: People who love to knit held a convention recently where they showed off their love for yarn and fiber arts.
item.content:encoded: People who love to knit held a convention recently where they showed off their love for yarn and fiber arts.
item.dc:creator: Leslie E. Thompson

item.title: Small, inexpensive drones are changing the battlefield. The Pentagon is playing catchup
item.description: As small attack drones become central to warfare, the Pentagon is making a major push to jumpstart manufacturing.
item.content:encoded: As small attack drones become central to warfare, the Pentagon is making a major push to jumpstart manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: The little-known DOJ division turning Trump's immigration policies into binding law
item.description: A little-known division inside the Justice Department that reviews immigration court appeals is turning President Trump's immigration policy agenda into law.
item.content:encoded: A little-known division inside the Justice Department that reviews immigration court appeals is turning President Trump's immigration policy agenda into law.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Amid a ceasefire in Lebanon, one family buries their daughter
item.description: Last week, NPR spent time with a family still searching for their 26-year-old daughter in the rubble one week after Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut. Now her remains have been identified.
item.content:encoded: Last week, NPR spent time with a family still searching for their 26-year-old daughter in the rubble one week after Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut. Now her remains have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Kennedy Center renovation tour
item.description: President Trump announced the Kennedy Center will close for two years beginning July 4th. He says the building is in bad shape and needs repairs. Members of congress and two lawsuits contend the real reason is mismanagement, artist cancellations and declining ticket sales.
item.content:encoded: President Trump announced the Kennedy Center will close for two years beginning July 4th. He says the building is in bad shape and needs repairs. Members of congress and two lawsuits contend the real reason is mismanagement, artist cancellations and declining ticket sales.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: More immigrants are being held in detention for over a year. NPR followed one family's ordeal.
item.description: The El Gamal family has been held in ICE detention for more than 320 days. They are not alone: the number of immigrants who've been in ICE detention for over a year has skyrocketed.
item.content:encoded: The El Gamal family has been held in ICE detention for more than 320 days. They are not alone: the number of immigrants who've been in ICE detention for over a year has skyrocketed.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd
